commands.review_summary:
The PR overlaps gateway startup logging, plugin service startup, sidecar startup, and channel startup while preserving sidecar readiness gating, with lifecycle tests for the new ordering.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction: this is a performance/refactor PR, and the intended ordering is source-reproducible from the diff plus the added lifecycle tests. The performance claim still needs branch-local real benchmark proof.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof on the exact split branch, not an automated code repair.

target.title: [Perf] Overlap gateway startup work before ready
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Gateway startup waited for some independent startup work serially before ready, even when ACPX probe semantics still required plugin services to complete before readiness.
- Why it matters: ACPX probe must stay default-on and awaited, but unrelated startup work can be overlapped to reduce restart ready latency without weakening the ready contract.
- What changed: Starts plugin services earlier, overlaps startup logging with sidecar startup, reports plugin service handles as soon as they are available, and keeps ready blocked until sidecars complete.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): ACPX probe remains enabled and awaited before ready. Channel startup, sidecar readiness, and startup log completion are still awaited before returning ready.
- AI-assisted: Yes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related: #82396
- Related: #82603
- Related: #79596
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Reduces Gateway restart ready latency by overlapping independent startup work while preserving ACPX readiness semantics.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS/Darwin arm64 checkout, Node v25.9.0. Full benchmark proof was collected on the combined local change set before splitting this PR series.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Targeted Gateway post-attach tests passed. The combined real benchmark artifact `.artifacts/gateway-restart-bench/acpx-probe-overlap-20260518.json` showed `skipChannelsAcpxProbe` `restart.ready` p50 moving from 812.2ms to 752.4ms with 0.0% failure rate.
- Observed result after fix: The startup scheduling contract is covered by tests, and the real combined benchmark showed about 59.8ms p50 restart-ready improvement with ACPX probe still enabled.
- What was not tested: The full real benchmark was not rerun on this split branch after review cleanup. Cross-OS and full CI were not run locally.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Pre-overlap benchmark on the same machine recorded `skipChannelsAcpxProbe` `restart.ready` p50 at 812.2ms.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Some startup work was serialized even though it did not need to block plugin service startup from beginning.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing tests checked ready ordering but did not assert that plugin services can start before channel startup completes or that startup logging can overlap sidecars.
- Contributing context (if known): ACPX probe must stay on the ready path because earlier fixes made Gateway ready wait for ACPX registration.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: sidecars start while startup logging is pending, plugin services start/report before channel startup completion, and ready still waits for sidecar completion.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The change is scheduling inside `startGatewayPostAttachRuntime` and `startGatewaySidecars`; focused lifecycle tests directly control pending promises and ordering.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing post-attach tests covered sidecar readiness but not this overlap ordering.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Gateway restart readiness can complete sooner in the ACPX-probe-on path. No defaults, config, permissions, or ACPX behavior change.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
startup log -> sidecars -> plugin services -> channels -> ready

After:
startup log -----------\
plugin services ----\   -> sidecars complete -> ready
channels ------------/
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Darwin arm64
- Runtime/container: Local Node v25.9.0 checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway startup sidecars and ACPX startup probe path
- Relevant config (redacted): `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, ACPX startup probe enabled for benchmark proof

### Steps

1. Run the targeted Gateway post-attach lifecycle tests.
2. Run core TS wrapper check.
3. Compare the combined local benchmark artifact before/after scheduling overlap.

### Expected

- Startup logging and plugin service startup can overlap independent work.
- Ready still waits for sidecar completion and ACPX startup probe semantics remain intact.

### Actual

- Targeted tests and core TS wrapper check passed.
- Combined benchmark showed lower `restart.ready` p50 in the ACPX-probe-on case.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [x] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: startup log overlap, plugin service early start/report, no unstaged work, core TS wrapper check.
- Edge cases checked: plugin service handle is reported before channel startup finishes so shutdown paths can receive the handle.
- What you did **not** verify: Full benchmark rerun on the split branch after review cleanup; cross-OS behavior.

Verification commands:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`: passed, 33 tests
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`: passed
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`: passed
- Combined benchmark before split: `node --import tsx scripts/bench-gateway-restart.ts --entry dist/entry.js --case skipChannelsAcpxProbe --case skipChannelsNoAcpxProbe --runs 1 --warmup 0 --restarts 5 --timeout-ms 30000 --output .artifacts/gateway-restart-bench/acpx-probe-overlap-20260518.json`: passed, 0.0% failure rate
- `codex review --base main`: attempted locally but tool-blocked; no clean Codex review verdict was produced.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

No existing PR review conversations were addressed by this PR.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: More concurrency in startup can hide ordering assumptions.
  - Mitigation: Added lifecycle tests for startup logging overlap, plugin services starting/reporting before channel completion, and ready still waiting for sidecar completion.
